subject: Product Management
title: MoSCoW Method
description: A quick win: understand MoSCoW Method.

hook.text: MoSCoW sorts features into four buckets: Must have, Should have, Could have, Won't have. The last one is the most important.

buildup.text: Dai Clegg invented MoSCoW at Oracle in 1994 for software development. It became popular in agile and product teams worldwide.

discovery.text: Must-haves are non-negotiable for the release. Should-haves are important but not critical. Could-haves are nice-to-haves.

twist.text: The 'Won't have' category is the real power. It explicitly documents what won't be built — preventing scope creep.

climax.text: MoSCoW works best with a rule: Must-haves should be 60% of effort. Should-haves 20%. Could-haves 20%.

punchline.text: Must. Should. Could. Won't. The last bucket is the discipline.

quiz.question: Why is the 'Won't have' category important in MoSCoW?
options: It explicitly documents what won't be built — preventing scope creep
It lists features that will be built next quarter
It's only relevant for waterfall projects
